rulings.Case Title: GCFS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. ANDREW A PETERSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: 2nd Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment; Request For Temporary Stay Of Enforcement; Request For Short Continuance Or, In The Alternative, Leave To Supplement, Pending Related Disciplinary/Agency Investigations; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Andrew Arthur Peterson; [Proposed]Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 6, 2026, Line 1, DEFENDANT ANDREW PETERSON 2nd Amended Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment; Request For Temporary Stay Of Enforcement; Request For Short Continuance Or, In The Alternative, Leave To Supplement, Pending Related Disciplinary/Agency Investigations. Off calendar at the request of the moving party. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: KRACHT ENTERPRISES III, LLC ET AL VS. CALIFORNIA GAMBLING CONTROL COMMISSION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 6, 2026, Line 2, RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA GAMBLING CONTROL COMMISSION, BUREAU OF GAMBLING CONTROL DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. Respondent's demurrer to the verified petition is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action and overruled as to the second and third causes of action. Petitioners bring claims related respondent the California Gambling Control Commission's ("Commission") administration and interpretation of the Gambling Control Act, Business & Professions Code, 19800 et seq. ("GCA.") (Subsequent citations are to the Business & Professions Code unless otherwise noted.) The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 6, 2026, Line 4, PLAINTIFF FLORENCE BONHAM's Motion To Substitute Charles Ernest Smith As Successor In Interest For Deceased Plaintiff Florence Bonham. rulings.Case Title: CLAUDIA GONZALEZ VS. SBM MANAGEMENT SERVICES, LP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Limiting Defendant'S Special Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions Against Defendant And His Counsel In The Amount Of $4,000.00
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 6, 2026, Line 8, PLAINTIFF CLAUDIA GONZALEZ's Motion For Protective Order Limiting Defendant's Special Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions Against Defendant And His Counsel In The Amount Of $4,000.00. The motion for protective order is denied. The court has reviewed the special interrogatories at issue and concludes that the complexity and numerosity of issues in this case warrants the additional special interrogatories that defendant has propounded. In particular, there are five defendants; there are seven causes of action in the complaint, and the complaint comprises 113 paragraphs. There is modest overlap in some interrogatories but they are distinct and the court concludes that the overlap is not burdensome or harassing. The court finds Plaintiff's meet and confer efforts to be adequate and the motion to be timely. Plaintiff shall respond to any interrogatories against which she asserted the 35-interrogatory limit by January 16, 2026. Both parties seek sanctions. "The court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion for a protective order under this section, unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2030.090, subd. (d).) The court does not find substantial justification and orders partial sanctions reflecting its view of a reasonable amount for this motion. Plaintiff shall pay $1500 to Defendant within 30 days of notice of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
